design: Cross-sectional 2nd-derivative of momentum across 30-coin weekly panel, dollar-neutral EW long-top/short-bottom, beta-neutral (BTC beta verified ~0 and hedged). Net of 4bps/side taker.
null: cross-sectional shuffle (permute signal->coin map per week; preserves realized return cross-section + dollar-neutrality)
strengthening_tried: DIFF (ROC-of-ROC) and SLOPE (short-minus-long drift) acceleration encodings
cross-sectional RESIDUAL acceleration (accel orthogonalized vs momentum each week) = THE separable-premium test
binding_gate: deflated_sharpe (also fails Harvey-Liu haircut and consume-once holdout)
interpretation: In-sample Sharpes up to 1.3 are entirely a multiple-testing artifact across ~96-138 configs (expected-max Sharpe under the null absorbs them; DSR p ~0.51). The XS-shuffle passes because SOME real cross-sectional dispersion is ranked, but the orthogonality test is decisive: acceleration residualized vs momentum has EXACTLY ZERO out-of-sample return (holdout Sharpe -0.0002). Acceleration adds no separable premium over plain momentum (itself already KILLed as T1/TA2); differencing amplified noise and raised the DSR bar exactly as the BACKLOG prior predicted. Decelerating-winners-reverse is outright negative in-sample.
